title: Mount Manipur National Park
tagline: Mount Manipur National Park — wildlife reserve of Andaman & Nicobar

heroImage.alt: Mount Manipur National Park, Andaman & Nicobar

overview.short: Mount Harriet National Park, officially renamed as Mount Manipur National Park, is a national park located in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands union territory of India.
overview.description: Mount Harriet National Park, officially renamed as Mount Manipur National Park, is a national park located in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands union territory of India. The park, established in 1969, covers about 4.62 km2 (18.00 mi2). Mount Manipur (Mount Harriet) (383 metres (1,257 ft)), which is a part of the park, is the third-highest peak in the Andaman and Nicobar archipelago next to Saddle Peak (732 metres (2,402 ft)) in North Andaman and Mount Thullier (568 metres (1,864 ft)) in Great Nicobar. The park is named in commemoration of Harriet C. Tytler, the second wife of Robert Christopher

topPlaces.name: Mount Manipur
topPlaces.description: Mount Manipur, formerly known as Mount Harriet, is the third highest peak in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands of India. The mountain houses the Mount Manipur Memorial, which commemorates the Manipuri freedom fighters of the Anglo-Manipur War and the Mount Manipur National Park (Mount Harriet National Park), which is best known for its rich biodiversity in the archipelago.

items.note: Mount Manipur, formerly known as Mount Harriet, is the third highest peak in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands of India. The mountain houses the Mount Manipur Memorial, which commemorates the Manipuri freedom fighters of the Anglo-Manipur War and the Mount Manipur National Park (Mount Harriet National Park), which is best known for its rich biodiversity in the archipelago.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mount Manipur National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mount Manipur National Park is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mount Manipur National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Veer Savarkar International Airport, Port Blair (19 km) and the nearest railway station is Puri Railway Station (1158 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mount Manipur National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mount Manipur National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mount Manipur National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Mount Manipur National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mount Manipur National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mount Manipur.
